current_focus: Track a Pareto frontier across reconstruction quality and sparsity/cost. Treat K=128 as one anchor point, not the only success criterion. Prefer experiments that add non-dominated tradeoff points, including smaller-K runs that accept some FVU increase when they improve the overall frontier.
architecture_findings: r1 topk no_change k? -> policy_reject (不允许 no_change；每一轮都必须提出可执行的实验或修复)

topk.design_hypothesis: `adam` at the signum-tuned LR regressed enough that the current `topk` mainline should revert to the stronger `signum` anchor and advance to the next stage in the recommended order: loss shaping. Under the now-tuned `signum` regime, the inherited `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125` auxiliary pressure may be over-regularizing support usage, so removing it could improve reconstruction curve shape and final FVU at `K=128` without changing LUTurbo compatibility.
next_steps: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` improves the curve and final FVU, keep the aux-free signum recipe as the new topk anchor before considering preprocessing.

If aux removal hurts late behavior or final FVU, restore `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125` and test a preprocessing change such as Hadamard only if the curve suggests representation conditioning is the next bottleneck.
If the loss/preprocess stage yields no gain, move to the K stage next and test whether a smaller `K` can offer a better LUTurbo tradeoff under the strongest confirmed topk recipe.

recent_rounds.hypothesis: With an empty EF=12 frontier, the highest-value Round 1 move is to establish a clean `topk` anchor at the policy reference recipe before any LR or optimizer sweep. A full baseline run at `ARCHITECTURE=topk K=128 EXPANSION_FACTOR=12 OPTIMIZER=signum LR=8e-4` will give the first trustworthy curve shape for this regime and prevent later tuning rounds from being confounded by an unverified starting point.
recent_rounds.termination_reason: 不允许 no_change；每一轮都必须提出可执行的实验或修复

recent_rounds.hypothesis: The exact clean baseline is not executable in this controller because `no_change` actions are policy-rejected, so the least-deviating Round 2 move is a single-variable `topk` learning-rate probe around the reference recipe. A modest LR increase from `8e-4` to `1.2e-3` may improve convergence speed and end-of-run FVU at `K=128` without changing the family, optimizer, or LUTurbo compatibility path.

The first policy-valid `topk` anchor at `LR=1.2e-3` established a usable K=128 EF=12 frontier point, and the next single-question mainline move is to test whether the signum recipe is still under-stepped. Increasing `LR` modestly again to `1.6e-3` may improve early descent and end-of-run FVU without changing the family, optimizer, or deployment form.

`topk` with signum has improved twice in a row as LR increased from `1.2e-3` to `1.6e-3`, so the current regime may still be under-stepped rather than saturated. A final modest LR increase to `2.0e-3` should answer whether the gain continues with stable curve shape or whether this branch is starting to overstep.

After three consecutive FVU improvements from raising signum LR up to `2.0e-3`, the clean next mainline question is whether optimizer choice, not just step size, is now the limiting factor for `topk` at `K=128`. Switching from `signum` to `adam` while holding the tuned recipe fixed will reveal whether a different update rule can improve reconstruction further or whether signum remains the stronger backbone optimizer in this EF=12 regime.

`adam` at the signum-tuned LR regressed enough that the current `topk` mainline should revert to the stronger `signum` anchor and advance to the next stage in the recommended order: loss shaping. Under the now-tuned `signum` regime, the inherited `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125` auxiliary pressure may be over-regularizing support usage, so removing it could improve reconstruction curve shape and final FVU at `K=128` without changing LUTurbo compatibility.

recent_insights: NOTE: Round 1 starts from an empty EF=12 frontier, so the first priority is a clean topk anchor rather than an exploratory family jump.
NOTE: The active mainline reference recipe is `ARCHITECTURE=topk K=128 EXPANSION_FACTOR=12 OPTIMIZER=signum LR=8e-4`.
NOTE: Use the resulting training curve shape, not just final FVU, to decide whether the next mainline move should be an LR increase, LR decrease, or optimizer change.
r1 topk no_change k? -> policy_reject (不允许 no_change；每一轮都必须提出可执行的实验或修复)
NOTE: The controller rejects exact clean-baseline submissions when encoded as `no_change`, so mainline progress must proceed through a policy-valid single-axis variant.
NOTE: Round 2 keeps `topk` fixed and starts the recommended LR stage with a modest upward step from the reference `8e-4` recipe.
NOTE: Judge this run by curve shape as well as final FVU: smoother early descent with continued late improvement would support staying on the higher-LR branch.
r2 topk param_only k128 -> keep fvu=0.4915001392364502
NOTE: Round 2 established the first EF=12 mainline frontier point for `topk` at `K=128`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.2e-3`, `FVU=0.4915001392364502`.
NOTE: Round 3 keeps the family fixed and continues the recommended LR stage with a modest upward step to `1.6e-3`.
NOTE: Evaluate this run by curve shape as well as final FVU: faster early drop with stable late improvement would justify keeping the higher-LR branch alive.
r3 topk param_only k128 -> keep fvu=0.46726518869400024
NOTE: The topk mainline improved from `FVU=0.4915001392364502` at `LR=1.2e-3` to `FVU=0.46726518869400024` at `LR=1.6e-3` under the same `K=128`, `EF=12`, `signum` regime.
NOTE: Round 4 continues the LR stage with one final modest upward step to `LR=2.0e-3` before considering an optimizer switch.
NOTE: Judge this run by curve shape as well as final FVU: sustained late improvement would justify the higher LR, while instability or late regression would mark the top of the useful LR range.
r4 topk param_only k128 -> keep fvu=0.4522051215171814
NOTE: The signum topk mainline improved monotonically across the LR sweep up to `LR=2.0e-3`, reaching `FVU=0.4522051215171814` at `K=128`, `EF=12`.
NOTE: Round 5 starts the optimizer stage by switching only `OPTIMIZER` from `signum` to `adam` while keeping the tuned topk anchor otherwise fixed.

NOTE: Interpret this run primarily by curve shape and stability versus the signum anchor: if `adam` is promising but mis-scaled, it should still show a plausible optimization trajectory worth one LR callback round.
r5 topk param_only k128 -> discard fvu=0.46458443999290466
NOTE: `adam` at `K=128`, `EF=12`, `LR=2.0e-3` underperformed the signum anchor (`0.46458443999290466` vs `0.4522051215171814`), so `signum` remains the current optimizer winner for topk mainline.
NOTE: Round 6 returns to the best signum anchor and starts the loss/preprocess stage with a single-variable probe on `AUXK_ALPHA`.
NOTE: Evaluate this run by curve shape and late behavior as well as final FVU: if aux-free training descends faster early but stalls or destabilizes late, keep the original aux loss.
r6 topk param_only k128 -> archive fvu=0.4522051215171814
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` improves the curve and final FVU, keep the aux-free signum recipe as the new topk anchor before considering preprocessing.

If aux removal hurts late behavior or final FVU, restore `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125` and test a preprocessing change such as Hadamard only if the curve suggests representation conditioning is the next bottleneck.
If the loss/preprocess stage yields no gain, move to the K stage next and test whether a smaller `K` can offer a better LUTurbo tradeoff under the strongest confirmed topk recipe.
